steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: A critic argues the infants were paying attention, not relaxing. The study found heart rate and pupil size both decreased. We need the fact that most weakens the attention explanation by showing decreased pupil size contradicts attention.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Parents of infants in the study preferred the Western Nahuatl lullaby over the Serbian non-lullaby.: Parental preference is irrelevant to whether decreased pupil size indicates attention or relaxation in infants. ✗
  • Choice B — Infants in the study had never heard the Western Nahuatl lullaby before.: Novelty might relate to attention, but does not address the physiological measure of pupil size that the critic must explain away. ✗
  • Choice C — More frequent blinking has also been found to be a reliable indication of attention.: Blinking was not measured in the study, so this cannot challenge the critic's interpretation of the pupil data. ✗
  • Choice D — Pupil size typically increases when a stimulus captures a person's attention.: If attention causes pupils to expand, then the observed constriction directly contradicts the critic's attention hypothesis, undermining their claim. ✓
That matches Choice D — Pupil size typically increases when a stimulus captures a person's attention..
